design_philosophy.core_loop: Aim, shoot, destroy targets with light gun peripheral; six mini-games with distinct mechanics (block-shooting, color-matching, whack-a-mole, missile interception)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Precision vs. reaction time; calibration accuracy directly impacts gameplay success
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Light gun arcade action brought home; operating a versatile targeting system across varied tactical scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hardware-dependent design philosophy: Super Scope 6 is uncompromisingly tied to the Super Scope peripheral. Game design entirely assumes light gun input; no fallback control scheme. Success hinges on physical calibration ritual and spatial accuracy. This is maximally opinionated about embodied, spatial gameplay over abstracted input.

difficulty.lives_system: Missile-based lives system in Intercept (lose on 5 missed missiles); time-pressure systems in other modes
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression in most games; Intercept mode spans 30 levels with no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Selectable difficulty settings (Low/Medium/High) determine starting stage in Blastris variants; player can choose game mode (Stage Game vs. Score Game) in Mole Patrol and Blastris B

progression.unlock_mechanism: Six discrete games arranged in two sub-menus (Blastris: 3 games; LazerBlazer: 3 game types); each game has independent progression
replayability_hooks: Score-chasing in Score Mode (Mole Patrol, Blastris B)
Two-player alternating modes in Blastris A and Intercept
High score boards per game type
Reaction-time bonuses reward precision and speed (Mole Patrol)

world_building_techniques: LazerBlazer communicator character (woman on screen) provides military-style mission briefings
Thematic naming: 'Intercept,' 'Engage,' 'Confront' suggest escalating combat scenarios
Mole Patrol garden-defense scenario provides loose thematic framing
Cross-game references: Mole Patrol mentions LazerBlazer Intercept mode (flying mole on rocket)

faction_indicators: LazerBlazer framing as military/defense operation (vs. unnamed threat)
Mole Patrol as civilian/rural protection (garden defense)
danger_signals: Time pressure and speed-up mechanics (red moles in Mole Patrol)
Fill-to-left-edge endgame condition in Blastris (mounting pressure)
environmental_storytelling: Garden setting in Mole Patrol (rural vulnerability)
Military command center aesthetic in LazerBlazer (woman communicator on screen)
Missile imagery and distance-judgment mechanics (space/depth warfare)
